page.content: In the period from 2020 to 2022, General Electric's total revenue increased from £1,200M in 2020 to £1,500M in 2022, with aftermarket service revenue growing from £300M to £420M, reflecting a growth rate of 25% in 2020, 20% in 2021, and 17% in 2022, while the market share rose from 15% in 2020 to 16% in 2021 and 17% in 2022.

lines.text: In the period from 2020 to 2022, General Electric's total revenue increased from £1,200M in 2020 to £1,500M in 2022, with
aftermarket service revenue growing from £300M to £420M, reflecting a growth rate of 25% in 2020, 20% in 2021, and 17% in
2022, while the market share rose from 15% in 2020 to 16% in 2021 and 17% in 2022.

page.content: The year-over-year change in revenue was 12.5% from 2020 to 2021, 11.1% from 2021 to 2022, and 25% from 2020 to 2022.

lines.text: The year-over-year change in revenue was 12.5% from 2020 to 2021, 11.1% from 2021 to 2022, and 25% from 2020 to 2022.

page.content: Regionally, the total service revenue comprised maintenance at £150M, overhaul at £100M, parts sales at £50M, and consulting at £30M, totaling £300M for services and £100M for parts. In the Industrial IoT metrics, units sold increased from 25,000 in 2020 to 45,000 in 2022, with connected devices growing from 100,000 to 300,000, and IoT revenue expanding from £80M to £120M. Furthermore, the headcount rose from 1,200 to 1,600, while maintenance costs and downtime hours improved significantly alongside efficiency increasing from 75% to 95%, energy consumption metrics demonstrated progress, and customer satisfaction climbed from 75% to 95%, with R&D investment also trending upward.
